title: atropis palustris
intro: Atropis palustris, also known as sweet gale or scotch moss, is a shrubby perennial plant native to Northern Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and diaphoretic effects, which help to relieve congestion and coughs. The health benefits of Atropis palustris include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote sweating to aid in the removal of toxins from the body. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
